kind of eventually, you know, was part of micro focus and whatnot.

Yeah, like, so we're raising 40 to 60 right now.
But for us, like, it's really about, like, finding the right partners more than anything.
Like, we're really capital efficient.
We burn 3 million a day total.
And so it's like, to get to 11.
And so I think, like, we're essentially going to be profitable.
In two months, again.
And so at that point, like, we don't need to raise.

It's like, but we are thinking of raising and seeing if it makes sense so that we can really double down on the platform and hire our engineering team even more aggressively and invest and go to market.
We just hire first AE, you know, so we haven't even invested in sales or marketing.
To get here is all word of mouth and organic.
Oh, wow.
And so it's like, we've done no outbound, no ads, nothing, you know.
And yeah, like.
So we're, but also like we got, we're not demand constraints.
So we're just like kind of heads down.
And, and so that's why we're like two, two, two weeks to just, cause we're getting so much inbound.
And, and then like, by the end of this week, we'll like kind of look at everything and decide like, okay, does it make sense to keep going?
And cause these processes are like all consuming.
And if not, like, we'll, we'll just go back to heads down execution.
And then like, you know, do go back to the original plan, which was like to probably raise next.
Yeah.
What do you look for?
feel like capital is almost a commodity.
Like, do you look for people with marketing tech expertise or yeah.
What do you, what areas do you guys need the most help with?
Yeah.

I'd say it's mostly people that understand services, software, but not trying to make this into a cheap SaaS or, or, you know, like ask any marketer in the planet, how many tools they have and how many tools they actually get.
And, you know, that's the problem, right?
Like, and then ask them how many freelancers they manage and how many agencies they've hired that they love working with, and you'll get your answer, right?
Like, and so, like, that's what we're solving more.
And so it's mostly people that understand, like, the services software kind of, like, concept and kind of how it's, like, just a different approach.
Like, I work with a lot of people early Palantir, right?
Like, you look at early Palantir and you probably would have been like, oh, this is not normal.
This is doesn't fit any pattern.
This is, you know, like, and so I think, like, but now everyone that invested in Palantir is like, oh, see, we knew it, right?
Like, and so, like, we're kind of in this phase where, like, things are shifting so quickly, trying to build the right primitives, you know?
And so we need investors that kind of understand that and are trying to, like, pattern matches against, like, the way companies were built before, you know?
Yeah, interesting.
Yeah, have you observed, obviously, you guys had a pioneer.

And, you know, in scaling this service as a product model, have you, I'm trying to think about, because I do think people are still forming their opinion and thinking through, you know, how this will look like in the future.
Do you have some thoughts about it?
Yeah, like, we have a few principles, which is like, one, you need to ride the model wave.
So anytime a new model comes out and gets better, it should make everything you do better, faster, cheaper, as fast as humanly possible.
So if like a new model comes out, and it takes you six months to refactor your UI and your products, and then train a bunch of your users to know how to use it, that's bad, right?
Like, so we're building towards that, like, which is like e-media value from new models.
And the second piece is like, every single output, every new customer, and every single run should make the whole better, cheaper, faster, right?
Which means you need to figure out how to learn from every human intervention, from every workflow, from every output, from every outcome.
And so you're creating a learn engine more than anything else, right?

Like, so the way I think about it, everything you're building is like this A-B testing framework almost of like taking the outcome, looking at the messy middle, which is like the experts plus AI workflows, taking it, looking at the input, looking at the results.
And it's just like creating an engine to figure out like what are the best recipes and blueprints to scale great work that drives the right outcomes for companies, right?
And I think companies just haven't figured that out.
And most teams we talk to, especially marketing teams, they're just copying and pasting  over to chat to BT and that's, you know, not going to scale, you know?
So we're trying to figure out like what is like cursor plus lovable with the right context engine plus Glean AI kind of look like or knowledge work outside of dev.
And, and, that's kind of like, we figure we want to build for services and expert led services because services, you start with the outcome and you work backwards from the outcome.
And if you don't have to deliver service.

One, get paid for it, but then the byproduct of doing that is like all this expert data that's like frontier data that can be used to fine-tune models, to create a reinforcement learning, to, you know, and there's a bunch of other things you can do with it.
Yeah, interesting.
Did you mention you are trying to block maybe two weeks to run the process?
So it sounds like you already met with some firms last week.
So are you trying to make a decision by this Friday?
Yeah, so we've had two partnership meetings.
We've got a couple more schedules.
Like we're mostly doing fourth, fifth, sixth meetings right now.
We have a data room and everything.
And so like we're, like I said, like we're keeping, we're not trying to artificially move the process any faster.
So we're not saying like we're not going to keep going with the process.
It's just mostly like on Friday, like my co-founder and I were like, let's just like recalibrate on Friday, figure out like, are we excited about these people?
Do they get it?
Like, do we want to do this?
this?
Do they Yeah.
Because it takes a lot from us and our time from the business.
And we're like getting so much demand from the business right now.
It's like, it's insane, you know?
So it's like, we just got to get back to work kind of thing, you know?
Yeah.
I think the next steps for us is if you can share the data room, I would love to share with a few folks on our team who has invested and involved with a lot of marketing tech companies.
And I think internally we have also been actively discussing what could the future of marketing look like?
And I would love to involve them into this discussion as well.
And then are you available Thursday, perhaps 8 a.m.?
Yeah.
Yeah, I can move some things around, yeah.
Okay.
Yeah, I would love to maybe have a follow-up conversation.
And hopefully, you know, once you send a data room, we'll dig into it today and tomorrow.
And then we will have a more prepared mind on Thursday.
Yeah, that sounds...
That sounds good.
Why don't I do this?
Let me send you the investment memo, which has like a ton, including our financials.
Based on that, like, I'll just wait for you all to kind of take a look at that and kind of understand.
And if you're like leaning in, then happy to open the and do the data room as well.
We're just like being very careful with like opening up our entire roadmap and everything.
I see.
Yeah.
And like kind of, unless people are truly like getting closer to the process and like leaning in, you know.
Yeah.
No, I don't blame you.
feel like, you know, sometimes the data room can be also very overwhelming for people who are not interested.
Right.
So you're just doing the qualifying process.
Yeah.
Yeah, exactly.
So I'll send you an investment memo that has a bunch of other things.
But then like we have a ton more to share as well after that.
Awesome.
Well, thank you so much, Marcel.
Yeah, I really enjoyed the conversation.
You guys are really the pioneers.
That's awesome.
Well, thank you so much.
We'll talk soon.
Yeah, take care.
Bye.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Explore potential investment opportunity in GrowthX, an AI-powered content marketing platform and service.](https://fathom.video/share/zrjxyLpxzQs4m1CKXdT9ZxrmTo8Zid7Q?tab=summary&timestamp=0.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [GrowthX combines AI workflows, expert-in-the-loop services, and a platform to deliver high-quality content marketing at scale](https://fathom.video/share/zrjxyLpxzQs4m1CKXdT9ZxrmTo8Zid7Q?tab=summary&timestamp=547.0)
  - [Currently at $11M ARR with 70% gross margins, raised $12M Series A in March](https://fathom.video/share/zrjxyLpxzQs4m1CKXdT9ZxrmTo8Zid7Q?tab=summary&timestamp=500.0)
  - [Exploring $40-60M Series B raise, but may opt to continue bootstrapping if right partners aren't found](https://fathom.video/share/zrjxyLpxzQs4m1CKXdT9ZxrmTo8Zid7Q?tab=summary&timestamp=495.0)
  - [Unique model blends software and services, targeting displacement of traditional marketing agencies](https://fathom.video/share/zrjxyLpxzQs4m1CKXdT9ZxrmTo8Zid7Q?tab=summary&timestamp=547.0)

## Topics

### Company Background & Traction

  - [Founded by Marcel Santilli, former marketing executive at IBM, HP, HashiCorp, Scale AI](https://fathom.video/share/zrjxyLpxzQs4m1CKXdT9ZxrmTo8Zid7Q?tab=summary&timestamp=250.0)
  - [Built content hubs/engines that drove significant revenue/traffic at previous companies](https://fathom.video/share/zrjxyLpxzQs4m1CKXdT9ZxrmTo8Zid7Q?tab=summary&timestamp=265.0)
  - [Started as workshops, evolved into service + platform model](https://fathom.video/share/zrjxyLpxzQs4m1CKXdT9ZxrmTo8Zid7Q?tab=summary&timestamp=452.0)
  - [Current metrics:](https://fathom.video/share/zrjxyLpxzQs4m1CKXdT9ZxrmTo8Zid7Q?tab=summary&timestamp=493.0)
      - [$11M ARR](https://fathom.video/share/zrjxyLpxzQs4m1CKXdT9ZxrmTo8Zid7Q?tab=summary&timestamp=516.0)
      - [70% gross margins](https://fathom.video/share/zrjxyLpxzQs4m1CKXdT9ZxrmTo8Zid7Q?tab=summary&timestamp=519.0)
      - [70+ employees (40 in delivery, rest in engineering/product)](https://fathom.video/share/zrjxyLpxzQs4m1CKXdT9ZxrmTo8Zid7Q?tab=summary&timestamp=1627.0)
      - [50+ customers including Ramp, Webflow, Surge AI](https://fathom.video/share/zrjxyLpxzQs4m1CKXdT9ZxrmTo8Zid7Q?tab=summary&timestamp=791.0)
      - [$9k-$200k/month contracts](https://fathom.video/share/zrjxyLpxzQs4m1CKXdT9ZxrmTo8Zid7Q?tab=summary&timestamp=1927.0)

### Product & Technology

  - [AI-powered workflow engine for content creation](https://fathom.video/share/zrjxyLpxzQs4m1CKXdT9ZxrmTo8Zid7Q?tab=summary&timestamp=547.0)
  - [Context engine ingests company info, audiences, competitors](https://fathom.video/share/zrjxyLpxzQs4m1CKXdT9ZxrmTo8Zid7Q?tab=summary&timestamp=573.0)
  - [Workflows for research, drafting, fact-checking, etc.](https://fathom.video/share/zrjxyLpxzQs4m1CKXdT9ZxrmTo8Zid7Q?tab=summary&timestamp=421.0)
  - [Analytics dashboard integrating LLM visibility, search console, GA](https://fathom.video/share/zrjxyLpxzQs4m1CKXdT9ZxrmTo8Zid7Q?tab=summary&timestamp=570.0)
  - [Forward-deployed experts for strategy and quality control](https://fathom.video/share/zrjxyLpxzQs4m1CKXdT9ZxrmTo8Zid7Q?tab=summary&timestamp=737.0)

### Business Model

  - [Initial 8-week strategy sprint ($20-30k)](https://fathom.video/share/zrjxyLpxzQs4m1CKXdT9ZxrmTo8Zid7Q?tab=summary&timestamp=1887.0)
  - [12-month growth execution contracts based on work streams](https://fathom.video/share/zrjxyLpxzQs4m1CKXdT9ZxrmTo8Zid7Q?tab=summary&timestamp=1891.0)
  - [Work streams include editorial, newsletters, programmatic pages, etc.](https://fathom.video/share/zrjxyLpxzQs4m1CKXdT9ZxrmTo8Zid7Q?tab=summary&timestamp=1895.0)
  - [Most customers $9k-$30k/month, some up to $200k+/month](https://fathom.video/share/zrjxyLpxzQs4m1CKXdT9ZxrmTo8Zid7Q?tab=summary&timestamp=1900.0)

### Market Opportunity

  - [Targeting 32,000 US marketing agencies, 300,000+ freelancers](https://fathom.video/share/zrjxyLpxzQs4m1CKXdT9ZxrmTo8Zid7Q?tab=summary&timestamp=1830.0)
  - [Aiming to be operating system for growth for top brands](https://fathom.video/share/zrjxyLpxzQs4m1CKXdT9ZxrmTo8Zid7Q?tab=summary&timestamp=1867.0)
  - [Seeing strong demand, all growth organic/word-of-mouth so far](https://fathom.video/share/zrjxyLpxzQs4m1CKXdT9ZxrmTo8Zid7Q?tab=summary&timestamp=2099.0)

### Fundraising & Future Plans

  - [Exploring $40-60M Series B raise](https://fathom.video/share/zrjxyLpxzQs4m1CKXdT9ZxrmTo8Zid7Q?tab=summary&timestamp=2053.0)
  - [Main goal is finding right partners who understand services+software model](https://fathom.video/share/zrjxyLpxzQs4m1CKXdT9ZxrmTo8Zid7Q?tab=summary&timestamp=2056.0)
  - [May continue bootstrapping if ideal partners not found](https://fathom.video/share/zrjxyLpxzQs4m1CKXdT9ZxrmTo8Zid7Q?tab=summary&timestamp=2118.0)
  - [Looking to accelerate platform development, expand engineering, invest in sales/marketing](https://fathom.video/share/zrjxyLpxzQs4m1CKXdT9ZxrmTo8Zid7Q?tab=summary&timestamp=2087.0)

### Competitive Differentiation

  - [Focus on codifying knowledge work within organizations](https://fathom.video/share/zrjxyLpxzQs4m1CKXdT9ZxrmTo8Zid7Q?tab=summary&timestamp=1751.0)
  - [Emphasis on learning from every interaction to improve system](https://fathom.video/share/zrjxyLpxzQs4m1CKXdT9ZxrmTo8Zid7Q?tab=summary&timestamp=1774.0)
  - [Ability to quickly leverage new AI models across entire workflow](https://fathom.video/share/zrjxyLpxzQs4m1CKXdT9ZxrmTo8Zid7Q?tab=summary&timestamp=1516.0)

## Next Steps

  - [Marcel to send investment memo with financials and additional details](https://fathom.video/share/zrjxyLpxzQs4m1CKXdT9ZxrmTo8Zid7Q?tab=summary&timestamp=2463.0)
  - [Rocio to review memo with F-Prime team and assess interest level](https://fathom.video/share/zrjxyLpxzQs4m1CKXdT9ZxrmTo8Zid7Q?tab=summary&timestamp=2412.0)
  - [Potential follow-up call scheduled for Thursday 8am if there's strong interest](https://fathom.video/share/zrjxyLpxzQs4m1CKXdT9ZxrmTo8Zid7Q?tab=summary&timestamp=2436.0)
  - [If proceeding, F-Prime to gain access to full data room for deeper diligence](https://fathom.video/share/zrjxyLpxzQs4m1CKXdT9ZxrmTo8Zid7Q?tab=summary&timestamp=2472.0)

action_items.description: Review GrowthX investment memo; share w/ F-Prime team members in marketing tech
Reschedule conflicting appointments for Thu 8am follow-up w/ Rocio
Send investment memo w/ financials to Rocio Wu
